data.title: Selvaag Bolig and Ferd Eiendom Partner for Major Moss Development
data.description:
Selvaag Bolig ASA (GB:0Q92) has released an update.

Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

Selvaag Bolig ASA has partnered with Ferd Eiendom to develop a major residential project in Moss, Norway, featuring approximately 800 homes with a projected sales value of five billion kroner. This collaboration aims to transform the area into a vibrant city space, incorporating the innovative Selvaag Pluss lifestyle concept. Both companies are optimistic about the project’s impact and potential, with sales anticipated to commence in 2026/2027.

For further insights into GB:0Q92 stock, check out TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: CaixaBank Advances in Share Buyback Program
data.description:
CAIXABANK (ES:CABK) has released an update.

Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

CaixaBank has actively executed its own share buyback program, purchasing a total of 9,954,819 shares between December 13 and December 19, 2024. This effort is part of a broader strategy, as the company has already bought back 43,729,780 shares, totaling approximately €228.9 million and representing 45.79% of the program’s maximum monetary amount. These transactions demonstrate CaixaBank’s commitment to enhancing shareholder value and strengthening its market position.

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

Aica Kogyo is enhancing its corporate governance by transitioning to a Company with an Audit and Supervisory Committee and establishing a Governance Committee led by outside directors. The company is also working to reduce cross-shareholdings and promote diversity in its workforce as part of its strategy to improve corporate value and ensure sustainable growth.

data.title: Spire Healthcare Executes Share Buyback to Enhance Shareholder Value
data.description:
Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

Spire Healthcare ( (GB:SPI) ) has provided an update.

Spire Healthcare Group PLC has executed a share buyback, purchasing 50,000 of its own ordinary shares on the London Stock Exchange as part of its buyback program announced in October 2024. The shares, purchased at prices ranging from 225.00 to 227.50 GBp, will be cancelled, affecting the total number of shares in issue and voting rights. This move is aimed at enhancing shareholder value and reflects Spire’s strategic financial management to maintain a healthy stock performance.

More about Spire Healthcare

Spire Healthcare is a leading independent healthcare group in the UK, operating 38 hospitals and over 50 clinics, offering services like private GP consultations and occupational health to over 800 corporate clients. It provided care to over 1 million patients in 2023 and is the leading private provider of knee and hip operations. Spire Healthcare is well-regarded, with 98% of its locations rated ‘Good’ or ‘Outstanding’ by health inspectors and is a member of the FTSE 250.

YTD Price Performance: 1.02%

Average Trading Volume: 525,370

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Hold

Current Market Cap: £913.9M

See more data about SPI stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: Motorpoint Appoints Swarupa Pathakji to Albion Technology Board
data.description:
Pick the best stocks and maximize your portfolio:

  • Discover top-rated stocks from highly ranked analysts with Analyst Top Stocks!
  • Easily identify outperforming stocks and invest smarter with Top Smart Score Stocks

An update from Motorpoint ( (GB:MOTR) ) is now available.

Motorpoint Group PLC has announced the appointment of Swarupa Pathakji as a Non-Executive Director of Albion Technology & General VCT plc, effective from December 19, 2024. This appointment follows the merger with Albion KAY VCT PLC and reflects Pathakji’s commitment to maintaining her responsibilities at Motorpoint. The announcement is part of Motorpoint’s continued focus on enhancing its governance structure while maintaining its market leadership in the vehicle retail industry.

More about Motorpoint

Motorpoint is the UK’s leading independent omnichannel vehicle retailer, providing customers with an accessible, affordable, and seamless way to buy, sell, and finance cars. It operates through its B2C platform Motorpoint.co.uk and a network of 21 stores, focusing on the nearly new car market, and includes an online wholesale platform, Auction4Cars.com, for B2B transactions.

YTD Price Performance: 19.21%

Average Trading Volume: 83,128

Technical Sentiment Consensus Rating: Buy

Current Market Cap: £116M

Metavisio SA successfully held its general meeting where all proposed resolutions, except one, were approved by the shareholders.
